title: Today's AI Brief: Claude's new model is more 'honest' when it messes up
summary: Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.8 model prioritizes honesty when making mistakes, while OpenAI explores self-improving tax agents with Codex.
imageAlt: Image from theverge.com for Claude’s new model is more ‘honest’ when it messes up
imageCaption: Claude’s new model is more ‘honest’ when it messes up (theverge.com)

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI briefing. Let's dive into some fresh news. First up, Anthropic's Claude is getting a bit more honest. Their new model, Opus 4.8, is launching on Thursday, and it's designed to be more upfront when it makes mistakes. I mean, who doesn't love a chatbot that can say 'oops, my bad'? It's a small but interesting step towards more trustworthy AI interactions.

According to The Verge, Opus 4.8 is Anthropic's effort to improve the model's ability to admit when it's unsure or doesn't know something. This is a big deal because, let's face it, AI models can be pretty confident even when they're wrong. It's like that one friend who always thinks they know the way, but really they're just lost. Anyway, it's cool to see Anthropic working on this.
Tax Agents Get an AI Boost

OpenAI is exploring the world of tax agents with Codex. They teamed up with Thrive and Crete to build a self-improving tax agent that can automate filings, improve accuracy, and speed up workflows. Imagine having a robot accountant that can actually keep up with tax season chaos. My read is that this could be a game-changer for tax prep, but we'll see how it plays out.

Claude’s new model is more ‘honest’ when it messes up, from theverge.com, is also worth a quick mention.
